mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth (GDPC1) reflects aggregate demand momentum. Sustained positive GDP growth indicates expanding economic activity, which supports corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-trading-day horizon.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC for GDPC1 vs 60-day forward returns is ≤ 0 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals labor market weakness, reducing consumer income expectations and spending, which depresses corporate profits and leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: After a UNRATE increase of >0.3pp over 3 months, the S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns more than 50% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: When the Fed cuts the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) while housing starts (HOUST) are also declining, the joint signal indicates underlying demand weakness that outweighs potential monetary stimulus, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS declines for 2 consecutive months AND HOUST declines for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: When both conditions hold simultaneously, S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive more than 50% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: In the post-recession recovery regime, rising industrial production (INDPRO) signals expanding manufacturing sector and improving supply conditions, which boosts corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-trading-day horizon.
failure_condition: In quarters officially designated as post-recession recovery, the rank correlation between INDPRO growth and subsequent 60-day S&P 500 returns is ≤ 0.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) indicates a strengthening labor market, which supports consumer spending and aggregate demand, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The rank correlation between PAYEMS month-over-month change and 20-day forward S&P 500 returns is non-positive over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Strong month-over-month growth in real retail sales (RSAFS) signals robust consumer demand, which translates into higher corporate revenues and earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: After a month with RSAFS growth >0.5%, the S&P 500 delivers negative 20-day returns more than 40% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous positive growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) and real retail sales (RSAFS) over a three-month period indicates synchronized labor market and consumer spending strength, reinforcing each other to boost corporate earnings and generate positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-trading-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: Both PAYEMS and RSAFS show positive 3-month growth
failure_condition: When both conditions hold, the 60-day forward S&P 500 return is negative more than 30% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: In a monetary tightening cycle, a consecutive three-month increase in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) signals further policy restriction that raises discount rates and depresses equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over a 5-trading-day horizon as markets quickly price in the tighter conditions.
failure_condition: During tightening cycles characterized by three consecutive FEDFUNDS increases, the 5-day forward S&P 500 return is positive more than 50% of the time.
